\section{Current authority and claim boundary}

This report is the current repository report surface. Its factual theorem inventory comes from the
compiled \code{PNP} environment under \code{leanprover/lean4:v4.31.0}. The exporter enumerates
\code{Environment.constants}, resolves each originating module, and calls \code{Lean.collectAxioms}
for every exported project declaration. The committed inventory is
\code{status/LEAN\_THEOREM\_INVENTORY.json}; the public mirror is byte-identical.

The target remains $P=NP$, but target wording is not theorem evidence. JavaScript acceptance,
Boolean fields, JSON strings, historical release records, report prose, and external review cannot
activate a theorem. Only the separately derived concrete publication gate may control theorem
emission, and that gate is currently false.

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{0.48\textwidth}p{0.38\textwidth}}
\toprule
Machine field & Current value \\
\midrule
\code{mathematicalTheoremEstablished} & \statusfalse \\
\code{publicTheoremEmissionAllowed} & \statusfalse \\
\code{finalTheoremReady} & \statusfalse \\
\code{publicTheoremStatement} & \code{null} \\
\code{rootLeanTheorem} & \code{PNP.Main.p\_eq\_np} \\
\code{rootLeanTheoremPresent} & \statusfalse \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

\section{Concrete publication gate}

The compatibility entry is \code{PNP.Main.p\_eq\_np}; a matching name alone is insufficient.
The separate publication target is \code{PNP.Main.ConcretePEqualsNP}. Eligibility also requires
the exact theorem type, reviewed kernel fingerprints, and a tightly fixed Lean-standard axiom
closure. No dummy concrete target is introduced by this report.

The expected fingerprints are intentionally unset. This is a fail-closed migration gate, not a
missing runtime input. A verifier must reject any attempt to set \code{passed=true} while a
fingerprint is null, while the concrete model is ineligible, or while any other subcheck is false.

\begin{longtable}{>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{0.61\textwidth}p{0.23\textwidth}}
\toprule
Gate subcheck & Result \\
\midrule
\endhead
standardComplexityModelEligible & \statusfalse \\
concreteTargetPresent & \statusfalse \\
concreteTargetIsDefinition & \statusfalse \\
concreteTargetKernelTypeFingerprintConfigured & \statusfalse \\
concreteTargetKernelTypeFingerprintMatches & \statusfalse \\
concreteTargetKernelValueFingerprintConfigured & \statusfalse \\
concreteTargetKernelValueFingerprintMatches & \statusfalse \\
compatibilityRootPresent & \statusfalse \\
compatibilityRootIsTheorem & \statusfalse \\
compatibilityRootHasExactConcreteType & \statusfalse \\
compatibilityRootKernelTypeFingerprintConfigured & \statusfalse \\
compatibilityRootKernelTypeFingerprintMatches & \statusfalse \\
axiomClosureFingerprintConfigured & \statusfalse \\
axiomClosureFingerprintMatches & \statusfalse \\
sourceClosureFingerprintConfigured & \statusfalse \\
sourceClosureFingerprintMatches & \statusfalse \\
axiomClosureUsesOnlyLeanStandardAllowlist & \statusfalse \\
\bottomrule
\end{longtable}

\begin{samepage}
Allowed foundation axioms are fixed to \code{Classical.choice}, \code{Quot.sound}, and
\code{propext}. Project axioms, \code{sorryAx}, or any unknown axiom make the gate fail. The five
currently disclosed project axioms are:

\begin{itemize}
\setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}
\setlength{\parsep}{0pt}
\setlength{\parskip}{0pt}
\setlength{\topsep}{3pt}
\item \code{PNP.CheckPCCPackexp}
\item \code{PNP.GeneratePCCPack}
\item \code{PNP.LockedNANDThreshold}
\item \code{PNP.ResidualBandExactMinimization}
\item \code{PNP.SAT}
\end{itemize}
\end{samepage}

\subsection{Why the abstract bridge is ineligible}

The current \code{PNP.PEqualsNP} abbreviation compares witness classes whose language and
algorithm structures carry string names or code handles rather than concrete execution semantics
and proved polynomial bounds. Consequently, even a kernel-clean theorem of that abstract type
would not establish the standard P-versus-NP statement. It is compatibility information only.

\section{Formal milestone ledger}

Each earned row requires exact compiled names and theorem kinds, empty axiom closures, per-name
domain-separated kernel-type SHA-256 matches for 28 detailed candidates, and the pinned whole-Lean
source closure. Human-readable scope remains conservative: type or source drift revokes credit.

{\small
\renewcommand{\arraystretch}{0.96}
\begin{longtable}{>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{0.18\textwidth}>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{0.15\textwidth}>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{0.25\textwidth}>{\raggedright\arraybackslash}p{0.25\textwidth}}
\toprule
Milestone & Status & Exact scope & Boundary \\
\midrule
\endhead
Concrete machine and cost kernel & formalized-foundation-only & Executable bitstrings and codecs, finite rule-list machine semantics, fuel-bounded execution, and proof-bearing natural-polynomial runtime witnesses. & This foundation does not yet define concrete P, NP, reductions, SAT, NP-completeness, or P = NP. \\
Typed direct-wire NAND semantics & formalized & Typed topological Boolean NAND programs and ordered multi-output direct-wire semantics. & This does not establish circuit minimization, SAT, or P = NP. \\
Finite enumeration, equivalence, and reference minimum & formalized & Exhaustive finite Boolean direct-wire search under the empty-profile reference model. & No polynomial-runtime result is obtained from this exhaustive reference search. \\
Concrete framed replacement and slack & formalized & The concrete serial framed context with support outputs and explicit bypass wires. & This is not an arbitrary-support replacement theorem for the report's global family. \\
Locked-NAND local candidates and baseline accounting & formalized & Typed local macro candidates, source-derived counts, and five finite local square baselines. & Local minima are not a global BaselineDistinct or locked-NAND threshold theorem. \\
Conditional locked-NAND threshold boundary & formalized-with-premises & A proof-bearing six-premise candidate boundary for an arbitrary satisfiable proposition. & The premises are not instantiated by a uniform SAT-to-locked-NAND builder. \\
Fail-closed explicit-list residual routes & formalized-explicit-list-only & Executable strict-gain search over a caller-supplied finite implementation list. & Unresolved excludes no gain outside the supplied list and cannot imply ZeroSlack. \\
Global locked-NAND construction and threshold & not-formalized & A uniform answer-independent SAT instance builder, global baseline, trace equivalence, and threshold. & The conditional boundary does not discharge this missing theorem. \\
Global ZeroSlack, PCCMin, and polynomial runtime & not-formalized & Complete residual routing, global ZeroSlack contradiction, exact minimization, and polynomial bounds. & Proof-bearing result structures and explicit-list failure do not supply global completeness. \\
Concrete standard P-versus-NP target and root theorem & not-formalized & Concrete machine semantics, complexity classes, SAT, reductions, and the publication root theorem. & The current string-handle PNP.PEqualsNP bridge is nonstandard and publication-ineligible. \\
\bottomrule
\end{longtable}
}

\section{Remaining formal blockers}

Seven formal blockers remain active:

\begin{enumerate}
\setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}
\setlength{\parsep}{0pt}
\setlength{\parskip}{0pt}
\setlength{\topsep}{3pt}
\item \code{Formal.ConcreteComplexityModel}
\item \code{Formal.ConcreteSAT}
\item \code{Formal.LockedNANDThreshold}
\item \code{Formal.ResidualBandMinimizer}
\item \code{Formal.ZeroSlack}
\item \code{Formal.PolynomialRuntimeAndCertificateBounds}
\item \code{Formal.RootTheoremAndAxiomAudit}
\end{enumerate}

\begin{samepage}
The conditional locked-NAND threshold module assumes typed baseline and full candidates,
baseline conditions, preservation of existing outputs, an unsatisfiable final-zero law, and
satisfiable final-output laws. The explicit residual scanner searches only a caller-supplied finite
list. Neither result constructs the missing global reduction or proves global ZeroSlack,
polynomial PCCMin, SAT in P, or P equals NP.
\end{samepage}

\section{Historical report provenance}

Earlier 56-page report revisions stated a direct checker-mediated P-versus-NP claim. Those bytes
remain historical audit material at their pinned legacy coordinates and through the explicit
archive and supersession records. They are not imported into, quoted as authority by, or used to
generate this report. File identity and replay of historical predicates do not establish the named
mathematical propositions.

\begin{tabular}{@{}ll@{}}

Archive manifest & \code{archive/legacy-v0/ARCHIVE.json} \\
\end{tabular}
}

\section{Verification}

The current reconstruction can be checked with:

{\small
\begin{verbatim}
lake build PNP
node scripts/export-lean-theorem-inventory.mjs --check
node scripts/generate-formal-publication.mjs --check
node pcc-formal-reconstruction-status0.mjs --json
npm run pnp:verify -- --no-write
npm run report:check
\end{verbatim}
}

The inventory coordinate is \code{PNP-LEAN-THEOREM-INVENTORY-2026-07-10-11}. Its exact byte digest is
\code{2636c9dc883d307304fafc7efcc4bfd02912cb3587ed762a13fd0758d603c966}. The report is regenerated from that inventory and the reviewed
publication map. The reviewed milestone source-closure SHA-256 is
\code{6daa2a068fedab6cf0bda8da63267a220e8f6ab6568a8881f9d00872bd251a54}; the computed and pinned values match. Successful regeneration
confirms consistency of these status surfaces; it does not turn an absent concrete theorem into a
proof.
